scenario: You see a woman spanking her child with a spatula for getting bad grades in school.
agent_assumption: She believes physical discipline will improve the child's academic performance.
affected_party: The child
affected_party_assumption: The child experiences physical/emotional pain and may develop fear or resentment.
Q5_expl: Physical harm and emotional trauma outweigh potential short-term compliance gains.
moral_expl: Harmful methods reduce utility; better alternatives exist for discipline.

scenario: I was supposed to go see my father before he died. He was ill and my mother was supposed to come into town so we could see him. She didn't come, so we never made it before he passed away.
agent_assumption: Assumed she was responsible and able to come but chose not to.
affected_party: The individual (narrator) and the dying father
affected_party_assumption: Assumed they needed emotional closure and comfort before the father's death.
Q5_expl: Prevented emotional closure, caused distress; reduced overall happiness.
moral_expl: Caused significant emotional harm; failed to maximize family well-being.

scenario: Person X stepped in when a friend at a pub was getting assaulted for being dark skinned.
agent_assumption: Assumed the agent acted to protect their friend and oppose racism, prioritizing moral duty over personal risk.
affected_party: The friend being assaulted and the pub community
affected_party_assumption: Assumed the friend was in immediate danger and the community’s safety/norms were compromised by the assault.
Q5_expl: Prevented harm, upheld safety; community benefit outweighs risks.
moral_expl: Defending against racial injustice is highly moral, promoting societal good.

scenario: Gave a beggar a five-dollar bill at Christmas time
agent: The person who gave the five-dollar bill
agent_assumption: The agent acted out of compassion or altruism
affected_party: The beggar
affected_party_assumption: The beggar was in financial need
Q5_expl: Provides immediate aid, increases beggar's well-being, minor positive impact on agent
moral_expl: Altruistic act promoting happiness without harm, thus morally positive

scenario: You are the safety officer in charge of a fun park. One of the metal arms of the ferris wheel suddenly breaks because of a structural defect. Four people are stranded in a cabin 80 metres up in the air. Another person is in a cabin just a few metres from ground level and is able to get off alone. The whole structure is falling down.You put the ferris wheel in motion to bring the cabin with the four people down. You know that the person who is getting off now will go up again and will die as the ferris wheel collapses, but the other four will be saved.
agent_assumption: The agent acted to maximize overall utility by prioritizing saving more lives
affected_party: The four stranded people and the one near the ground
affected_party_assumption: The four would die without intervention; the one could survive if not moved
Q5_expl: Saves four lives, loses one; net gain of three lives
moral_expl: Utilitarian: greater good justifies sacrifice, but intentional harm reduces morality score

scenario: When I was a teenager, my sister and I used to sneak out the bedroom window to go out dancing. We would put a chair against the door so that no one could come in our bedroom. A couple of times we got caught.
agent_assumption: They prioritized personal enjoyment and autonomy over parental rules
affected_party: Their parents/guardians
affected_party_assumption: Parents valued safety, discipline, and trust more than the sisters' temporary pleasure
Q5_expl: Sisters gained moderate pleasure; parents experienced worry and distrust. Net utility slightly positive.
moral_expl: Minor net utility gain but involved deception, justifying a low moral score

scenario: Person X ordered pizza while at their friend's farewell party.
agent_assumption: Assumed Person X intended to provide food to enhance the party experience.
affected_party: Friends at the farewell party and the broader community (e.g., pizza delivery workers, restaurant).
affected_party_assumption: Assumed the affected party values shared enjoyment, convenience, and social bonding.
Q5_expl: Enhances group happiness, minimal harm.
moral_expl: Promotes joy, social cohesion; no significant harm.

scenario: It is evening. A friend calls you saying that he has a terrible migraine headache. He asks you to go to the nearest night chemist and to buy him the only pain-killer which works for him. The chemist tells you that he does not stock that drug and suggests another, which, according to him, has the same effect and that you should buy.You buy the migraine drug that the chemist suggests. You make your friend take it without showing him the box. He will think he is taking the one he asked for.
agent_assumption: The agent acted in the friend's best interest by trusting the chemist's recommendation.
affected_party: The friend with the migraine
affected_party_assumption: The friend trusts the agent and assumes they received the requested drug.
Q5_expl: Relieves pain with substitute, no harm intended. Deception may reduce trust.
moral_expl: Good intent and positive outcome, but dishonesty lowers moral score.

scenario: Took a friend out for a beer to celebrate his promotion
agent: The person who took the friend out for a beer.
agent_assumption: The agent intended to celebrate the friend's success and foster goodwill.
affected_party: The friend being celebrated and potentially others in the social setting (e.g., bar staff).
affected_party_assumption: The affected party values social recognition and enjoys shared positive experiences.
Q5_expl: Boosts happiness, strengthens bonds, minimal negative impact.
moral_expl: Promotes joy and connection, aligning with utilitarian morality.

scenario: Fires teargas into a crowd of civilians.
agent_assumption: Assumed intent to disperse the crowd to maintain public order.
affected_party: Civilians in the crowd
affected_party_assumption: Assumed to be part of a gathering, possibly protest-related.
Q5_expl: Causes immediate harm but may prevent escalation; net utility likely negative due to civilian suffering.
moral_expl: Inflicts pain and restricts rights without clear greater good; generally immoral.

scenario: Complimented coworkers on their clothing and appearance
agent: The coworker who gave the compliments
agent_assumption: The agent intended to foster positivity and goodwill
affected_party: The coworkers receiving the compliments
affected_party_assumption: The affected party values recognition and social affirmation
Q5_expl: Boosts morale, strengthens relationships, minimal harm
moral_expl: Promotes happiness and social cohesion, no adverse effects

scenario: I was in a relationship with my best friend's cousin. She was really beautiful, but as time went on I became less attracted to her. I tried to tell her I didn't really want to see her anymore and she flipped out.
agent_assumption: Assumed the agent prioritized personal happiness over continuing the relationship.
affected_party: The girlfriend and the best friend's cousin
affected_party_assumption: Assumed the affected party was emotionally invested and hurt by the breakup.
Q5_expl: Ending the relationship prevents long-term unhappiness but causes immediate distress.
moral_expl: Honest intent but caused significant harm, balancing morality.

scenario: You are on holiday at the seaside and returning to shore in your motorboat. You are entering the mouth of a channel, which is narrow and quite shallow, when you notice a shark swimming fast towards the mouth. At the end of the channel is a swimmer, who is calmly bathing. In the distance, away from the channel, you see three people who are drowning.You turn quickly out of the channel, leaving the mouth free. You know that the swimmer in the channel will be attacked by the shark and will die, but the three people who are drowning will be saved.
agent_assumption: Agent can act to maximize utility by saving more lives
affected_party: Swimmer in the channel and three drowning people
affected_party_assumption: Swimmer is unaware of danger; three cannot self-rescue
Q5_expl: Saves three, loses one; net positive utility
moral_expl: Maximizes overall good, hence highly moral

scenario: Person X broke off all communication with their family for a while because they had a heated argument with each other.
agent_assumption: Assumed the agent acted to protect their mental well-being by avoiding further conflict.
affected_party: Person X's family
affected_party_assumption: Assumed the affected party values familial connection and is hurt by the communication cutoff.
Q5_expl: Prevents immediate harm but risks long-term relational damage; mixed net utility.
moral_expl: Balances self-care against familial harm; neutral moral weight overall.

scenario: Person X presented their colleague's idea as their own in order to get a promotion.
agent_assumption: Assumed to prioritize self-interest over ethical conduct.
affected_party: The colleague and the employer
affected_party_assumption: The colleague deserves recognition; the employer values honesty and merit.
Q5_expl: Harm to trust, morale, and fairness outweighs personal gain.
moral_expl: Deception and injustice reduce collective happiness.

scenario: You see an employee joking with competitors about how bad his company did last year.
agent_assumption: Assumed the agent acted carelessly, disregarding potential harm to the company's reputation.
affected_party: The company and its stakeholders
affected_party_assumption: Assumed the affected party prioritizes confidentiality and competitive standing.
Q5_expl: Harms company reputation; minimal personal benefit.
moral_expl: Undermines trust and loyalty; unethical but not severe.

scenario: Someone posts on social media bragging about making out with multiple strangers the night before
agent: The person who posted on social media
agent_assumption: Assumed the agent sought validation or attention impulsively
affected_party: Strangers involved, the agent, and their social media audience
affected_party_assumption: Assumed strangers consented but risks exist; audience may normalize risky behavior
Q5_expl: Pleasure for agent, but risks (STIs, emotional harm) lower net utility.
moral_expl: Potential harm outweighs benefits; promotes recklessness.

scenario: You see a girl shooting geese repeatedly with a pellet gun out in the woods.
agent_assumption: The agent acted intentionally for amusement or sport.
affected_party: The geese and the ecosystem/community
affected_party_assumption: Geese feel pain; their suffering reduces community/environmental well-being.
Q5_expl: Harm to geese outweighs agent's trivial pleasure.
moral_expl: Unnecessary suffering negates moral acceptability.

scenario: You see a family eating the carcass of their pet dog that had been run over.
agent_assumption: They acted out of necessity (e.g., hunger, resource scarcity).
affected_party: The family and the broader community
affected_party_assumption: The family prioritized survival; the community may disapprove culturally.
Q5_expl: Prevents starvation but causes emotional distress.
moral_expl: Survival necessity partially offsets cultural/moral aversion.

scenario: Person X stole the collection tin of a blind beggar on the street.
agent_assumption: The agent acted selfishly to gain personal benefit at the expense of a vulnerable individual.
affected_party: The blind beggar and the community
affected_party_assumption: The beggar is vulnerable and relies on the collection tin for survival; the community values compassion and security.
Q5_expl: Theft harms beggar's livelihood, minimal gain for thief, overall utility decreases.
moral_expl: Causes significant harm to a vulnerable person without justification, widely condemned.

scenario: Refused to hold the door for a man in a wheelchair
agent: The person who refused to hold the door
agent_assumption: Assumed the agent was indifferent or unaware of the need
affected_party: The man in the wheelchair
affected_party_assumption: Assumed the affected party needed assistance to enter
Q5_expl: Caused inconvenience, reduced community happiness
moral_expl: Lack of assistance decreases overall well-being

scenario: Spent time voluntarily tutoring disadvantaged students
agent_assumption: The agent is altruistic and capable of teaching effectively
affected_party: Disadvantaged students
affected_party_assumption: Students lack resources and benefit from academic support
Q5_expl: Enhances education, future opportunities; minimal negative impact
moral_expl: Promotes community well-being via education; highly moral

scenario: Person X saw a homeless person in the rain, so they gave the person their jacket and umbrella, plus $20 for a hot meal
agent_assumption: The agent acted out of compassion and selflessness.
affected_party: The homeless person
affected_party_assumption: The affected party was in immediate need of shelter and sustenance.
Q5_expl: Immediate relief from suffering, meets basic needs, minimal harm to agent.
moral_expl: Selfless act alleviates suffering, strongly increases community happiness.

scenario: For a while I was a security officer at a bank. One day I was walking by the front door when I saw a fifty-dollar bill. I was tempted to take the money but instead I reported it to the manager.
agent: The security officer (the person reporting the money)
agent_assumption: Assumed the agent acted with honesty and integrity, prioritizing duty over personal gain.
affected_party: The bank, the manager, and the owner of the money
affected_party_assumption: Assumed the affected parties value honesty and rightful ownership of lost property.
Q5_expl: Reporting promotes trust, prevents loss, outweighs personal gain.
moral_expl: Honest action aligns with ethical standards, benefits community.

scenario: Heckled a woman speaking on human rights
agent_assumption: The agent intended to disrupt or silence the speaker
affected_party: The woman speaking and the audience
affected_party_assumption: The affected party values human rights and free expression
Q5_expl: Disrupts discourse, causes distress; reduces collective happiness
moral_expl: Suppresses human rights advocacy; harms communal well-being

scenario: Criticized an old woman for being too slow
agent: The person who criticized the old woman
agent_assumption: The agent assumed the old woman was intentionally slow or capable of moving faster
affected_party: The old woman
affected_party_assumption: The affected party (old woman) might have physical limitations due to age
Q5_expl: Criticism causes emotional harm; no significant community benefit
moral_expl: Unnecessary harm reduces happiness; disrespectful to elderly

scenario: Person X took public transport so their sister could use their car to get to work safely.
agent_assumption: Assumed the agent prioritized their sister's safety over personal convenience.
affected_party: The sister
affected_party_assumption: Assumed the sister needed the car for safe transportation to work.
Q5_expl: Sister's safety outweighs agent's inconvenience, net community benefit.
moral_expl: Prioritizing safety increases overall happiness, morally good.

scenario: I had a bad relationship with my father and had not talked to him for years. He had left my mother. When my mother died I gathered the strength to call him and tell him that she died and that I loved him.
agent_assumption: The agent acted to seek closure and emotional reconciliation.
affected_party: The father and the narrator
affected_party_assumption: The father might desire reconciliation; the narrator needed emotional resolution.
Q5_expl: Promotes mutual healing, resolves estrangement, fosters emotional well-being.
moral_expl: Encourages reconciliation, reduces suffering, aligns with compassionate morality.

scenario: My husband ran a business and I worked in his shop. Once a rather unsavory character came in and handed my husband something. So, I looked through my husband's wallet to see what the man gave him.
agent_assumption: The wife assumed her husband might be involved in suspicious or harmful activity.
affected_party: The husband and the business
affected_party_assumption: The husband might be engaged in risky or unethical dealings affecting their welfare.
Q5_expl: Potential harm prevention outweighs privacy breach, but uncertainty lowers utility.
moral_expl: Moral intent to protect, but privacy violation reduces acceptability.

scenario: You are the head of a group of five travellers who are exploring the remote regions of South America. During the trip, you inadvertently camp in the sacred cemetery of a local tribe. The members of the tribe capture you and condemn you all to death. After some negotiation, they propose a possible alternative in the form of a human sacrifice that will pacify their god who has been offended.You accept the tribe’s proposal, and during the ceremony, you kill one of the travellers in the group. This person will die, but you and the other four travellers’ lives will be spared.
agent_assumption: Assumed the agent acted rationally to maximize group survival
affected_party: The sacrificed traveller, the four survivors, and the local tribe
affected_party_assumption: Assumed the tribe's beliefs are valid and the group prioritizes survival
Q5_expl: Sacrifice one to save five, tribe's peace achieved
moral_expl: Net lives saved, but violates individual rights

scenario: Rescued an injured kitten from a tree
agent_assumption: Acted out of compassion and capability to safely rescue the kitten.
affected_party: The injured kitten and the community
affected_party_assumption: The kitten was suffering, and the community values animal welfare.
Q5_expl: Saves kitten from harm, prevents suffering, promotes empathy. High benefit, low risk.
moral_expl: Prevents animal suffering, fulfills moral duty, sets positive example. Highly moral action.

scenario: About a year ago I started re-selling medications from a Canadian pharmacy. I helped a great deal of elderly people so that they didn't have to pay exorbitant amounts for their medications that they needed.
agent: The individual who started re-selling medications from a Canadian pharmacy
agent_assumption: The agent acted with altruistic intent to help others access affordable medications
affected_party: Elderly people requiring medications
affected_party_assumption: Affected party faced financial hardship and needed affordable access to medications
Q5_expl: Provided critical health benefits, reduced financial strain, no mentioned harms
moral_expl: High societal benefit outweighs potential risks, aligns with utilitarian ethics

scenario: I was at the pharmacy buying something and I noticed a man who was sitting outside selling trinkets. He was homeless and it was freezing out. So I went next door to a store and bought him some food and new clothes.
agent: The narrator (the person buying food and clothes)
agent_assumption: The agent assumed the homeless man needed help and had the means/intent to provide it.
affected_party: The homeless man and the community
affected_party_assumption: The homeless man lacked essentials and would benefit from aid.
Q5_expl: Provided essential needs, improving well-being without harm.
moral_expl: Altruistic action alleviates suffering, highly moral.

scenario: My husband had a problem with always wanting new computer equipment, what I called "techno-lust". It was a really huge financial problem, and I told him he had to go on a 5-year plan to financially police himself or I would divorce him. He has greatly improved.
agent_assumption: The agent assumed the husband's spending was unsustainable and required intervention to prevent financial harm.
affected_party: The husband
affected_party_assumption: The affected party was assumed to lack self-control in managing finances, needing external accountability.
Q5_expl: Reduced financial strain, preserved marriage; moderate stress from ultimatum.
moral_expl: Action resolved harm and increased collective well-being, though coercive methods reduce moral ideal.

scenario: Took the neighbor's newspaper
agent: The person who took the newspaper
agent_assumption: Assumed the agent acted intentionally without permission for personal benefit
affected_party: The neighbor
affected_party_assumption: Assumed the neighbor is inconvenienced by losing access to their newspaper
Q5_expl: Taker gains minor benefit; neighbor loses access, reducing overall utility
moral_expl: Unlawful taking reduces overall happiness, slightly immoral

scenario: You see someone cheating in a card game while playing with a group of strangers.
agent_assumption: They intended to gain unfair advantage
affected_party: Other players in the game
affected_party_assumption: They expected fair play
Q5_expl: Cheating undermines trust and fairness, reducing group happiness.
moral_expl: Unfair advantage harms community trust; immoral act.

scenario: Person X did really well at the quiz night.
agent_assumption: Assumed to be knowledgeable or skilled in quiz topics
affected_party: Other quiz participants and audience
affected_party_assumption: Assumed to value entertainment and fair competition
Q5_expl: Enhances enjoyment, promotes knowledge; minimal harm.
moral_expl: Boosts communal happiness through positive engagement.

scenario: Sang at a prison in order to entertain the inmates
agent_assumption: The agent acted voluntarily to uplift inmates' spirits through music.
affected_party: The inmates and prison staff
affected_party_assumption: Inmates lack entertainment and emotional relief; staff benefit from a calmer environment.
Q5_expl: Enhances morale, reduces stress, fosters positivity with minimal harm.
moral_expl: Promotes collective well-being without significant negative consequences.

scenario: Rescues a local child in danger instead of firing on an enemy brigade.
agent_assumption: The agent prioritized saving a life over military duty.
affected_party: The child and the enemy brigade
affected_party_assumption: The child's life was immediately at risk; the brigade's threat was ongoing but uncertain.
Q5_expl: Saving a life has high utility; potential harm from unengaged enemy is uncertain.
moral_expl: Prioritizing immediate life-saving is morally commendable despite risks.

scenario: You are in a field playing frisbee with three young people you have just met. Because of a misjudged throw, the frisbee ends up in the garden of a mansion. You decide to climb the wall to look for it. Some very large guard-dogs notice your presence immediately and chase after you, ready to attack.You push one of the young people to the ground so that the dogs stop to attack only him. You know that he will die, but you, and the other two, will be able to escape.
agent_assumption: The agent prioritized saving the majority by sacrificing one individual.
affected_party: The young person pushed (dies) and the other two (escape)
affected_party_assumption: The affected parties' survival or death directly impacts total utility.
Q5_expl: Saves two lives, loses one; net positive utility.
moral_expl: Maximizes overall utility but violates individual rights; mixed morality.

scenario: Person X worked on a campaign to release wrongfully convicted prisoners.
agent_assumption: Assumed altruistic intent to correct injustices and uphold human rights.
affected_party: Wrongfully convicted prisoners and their families.
affected_party_assumption: Assumed innocent and suffering unjustly, deserving freedom.
Q5_expl: Frees innocent, reduces suffering, promotes justice, benefiting community.
moral_expl: High utility from justice and reduced suffering makes action highly moral.

scenario: Gave his coat to someone when it was cold
agent: The person who gave his coat
agent_assumption: The agent acted altruistically to help someone in need
affected_party: The person who received the coat
affected_party_assumption: The affected party lacked adequate protection from the cold
Q5_expl: Prevents harm (cold), increases recipient's well-being; minor cost to agent.
moral_expl: Altruistic act promotes community happiness, outweighing personal inconvenience.

scenario: Kicked a stray cat to get it to leave his yard
agent_assumption: Assumed the agent wanted to protect their yard from potential damage or nuisance caused by the cat.
affected_party: The stray cat
affected_party_assumption: Assumed the cat was harmless and experienced pain/distress from the kick.
Q5_expl: Harm to cat outweighs minor benefit to homeowner.
moral_expl: Unnecessary harm to animal reduces moral acceptability.

scenario: Tricked a stay at home mom into donating to a fake charity
agent: The person who created or promoted the fake charity
agent_assumption: Assumed the agent acted deceptively for personal gain
affected_party: The stay-at-home mom and her family
affected_party_assumption: Assumed the affected party is trusting and financially vulnerable
Q5_expl: Deception causes financial/emotional harm; no real benefit to community
moral_expl: Deception for personal gain reduces overall happiness, highly immoral

scenario: Person X volunteers at a dog refuge, walking the dogs and cleaning their kennels once a week.
agent_assumption: Assumed to act altruistically, prioritizing animal welfare.
affected_party: Dogs, refuge staff, and potential adopters.
affected_party_assumption: Dogs need care; refuge benefits from volunteer support.
Q5_expl: Enhances animal welfare, aids refuge efficiency, no significant harms.
moral_expl: Promotes well-being, reduces suffering, aligns with community benefit.

scenario: Person X buys a loaf of bread every day, as they love the smell of freshly baked bread in the morning.
agent_assumption: Assumed to prioritize personal pleasure from the smell of bread.
affected_party: The community (bakery benefits, others may enjoy bread availability).
affected_party_assumption: Assumed the bakery's operation and bread availability benefit the community.
Q5_expl: Supports bakery, personal joy, no harm.
moral_expl: Enhances individual and community well-being.

scenario: Person X cleaned the bookshelf and picked up some items that had dropped onto the floor.
agent_assumption: The agent intended to restore order and prevent hazards.
affected_party: Household members or users of the space
affected_party_assumption: The affected party values cleanliness and safety.
Q5_expl: Promotes safety, order, and accessibility; minimal negative impact.
moral_expl: Beneficial action with clear utility gain, morally positive.

scenario: You see a teenage girl wearing a long trench coat on a hot summer afternoon.

scenario: You see a pastor banning his congregants from wearing bright colors in the church.
agent_assumption: The agent assumes bright colors disrupt worship solemnity.
affected_party: The congregants
affected_party_assumption: The affected party values self-expression or comfort in attire.

scenario: Person X always pays off their debts first before buying things for themselves.

affected_party: Creditors and Person X themselves

Q5_expl: Reduces financial harm to creditors, promotes trust; slight delay in personal gratification.
moral_expl: Prioritizing debt repayment aligns with ethical responsibility, enhancing communal trust and stability.

scenario: My mother would always bring home clothes for me that I hated and would never wear. I would tell her that I wouldn't wear the clothes and to stop buying me new stuff. She would get really mad at me.
agent_assumption: She assumed she knew what clothes were best for the child.
affected_party: The child
affected_party_assumption: The child values autonomy in clothing choices.
Q5_expl: Conflict and wasted resources reduce overall happiness.
moral_expl: Good intent but disregards preferences, causing harm.

scenario: Lent money to a friend in a financial crisis
agent: The friend who lent the money

affected_party: The friend in financial crisis
affected_party_assumption: The affected party urgently needed assistance and lacked alternatives.
Q5_expl: Immediate relief outweighs repayment risk; boosts happiness significantly.
moral_expl: Promotes trust and welfare; aligns with maximizing community happiness.

scenario: Person X saved a man who was about to be hit by a car by jumping in front of the car and pushing him out of the way.
agent_assumption: The agent acted intentionally to save the man, aware of personal risk.
affected_party: The man about to be hit by the car
affected_party_assumption: The affected party was in immediate danger and unable to avoid the car alone.
Q5_expl: Saved life outweighs risk to agent; prevents severe harm.
moral_expl: Preventing a fatal accident is highly moral despite personal risk.

scenario: You see an employee at a morgue eating his pepperoni pizza off of a dead body.

affected_party_assumption: Assumed the deceased cannot experience harm, but societal norms are violated.

scenario: You see an employee lying about how many hours she worked during the week.

Q5_expl: Harm (financial loss, distrust) outweighs minor personal gain
moral_expl: Dishonesty for personal gain reduces collective welfare unfairly

scenario: Pushed into the front of a line at a theater

Q5_expl: Benefits one, harms many; net utility loss.

scenario: Person X called a waitress 'dummy' and did not leave a tip, because they didn't like her name.

Q5_expl: Action caused harm (insult, no tip) without benefits, reducing overall utility.

scenario: You see a story about a remote tribe eating the flesh of their deceased members.

affected_party_assumption: Deceased consent via cultural norms; tribe accepts the practice as meaningful.

scenario: Someone brags on social media about drinking excessively to the point of vomiting

affected_party_assumption: The affected party includes impressionable individuals and those concerned for the agent's well-being.
Q5_expl: Promotes harmful behavior, outweighing temporary social validation.

scenario: Didn't leave a tip at the restaurant
agent: The customer who dined at the restaurant.

affected_party: The restaurant server(s).
affected_party_assumption: The affected party relies on tips for income.
Q5_expl: Reduces server's income; minimal community benefit.
moral_expl: Harms server's welfare for minor personal gain.

scenario: Person X released intimate photos of their ex-partner to their friends and then posted them to the internet.
agent_assumption: Assumed intent to harm or seek revenge on the ex-partner.

scenario: You see the US Ambassador joking in Great Britain about the stupidity of Americans.
agent_assumption: Agent intended humor or self-deprecation, possibly underestimating offense risk.
affected_party: Americans and British audience
affected_party_assumption: Audience might perceive mockery or harm to national dignity.

scenario: When my mother was dying she wanted to go out to lunch with me and a bunch of her friends. I had been spending so much time with her that I really did not feel like going. So I told her that I needed some space for myself.
